articles.title: Are Cardi B and Stefon Diggs back together? New family photo raises questions about relationship status
articles.description: Cardi B and Stefon Diggs were photographed together backstage during her "Little Miss Drama Tour," with Diggs holding their infant son as Cardi prepared to perform. The image has fueled reconciliation speculation after the two reportedly split earlier this year. According to prior reporting, the breakup was driven by trust issues, with Cardi prioritizing her children and career. Neither party has confirmed a reunion, though sources previously suggested co-parenting remains ongoing and a future reconnection is possible.

articles.title: 'KD – The Devil' box office collections day 1: Dhruva Sarja and Sanjay Dutt's action film collects Rs 3.50 cr
articles.description: Dhruva Sarja's action-packed 'KD – The Devil' has opened to a modest Rs 3.50 crore net on its first day nationwide. Despite a strong star cast including Sanjay Dutt and Shilpa Shetty, the film garnered a 46% overall occupancy. Early reviews suggest Dhruva Sarja's performance as Kali is a highlight in this large-scale commercial entertainer.

articles.title: Candace Owens retweets Charlie Kirk's old tweet as Jimmy Kimmel controversy turns against Donald Trump
articles.description: A resurfaced post by Charlie Kirk has intensified a dispute involving Donald Trump, Melania Trump, and Jimmy Kimmel. Backed by Candace Owens, the message reignited debate over free speech after Kimmel’s controversial joke. While Trump pushed for action, Kimmel defended satire and First Amendment rights, turning the controversy into a wider discussion on speech, accountability, and public standards.

articles.title: 'Bhooth Bangla' box office collection Day 14: Akshay Kumar's film BREACHES Rs 200 crore worldwide mark at the end of week 2
articles.description: In a thrilling cinematic accomplishment, Akshay Kumar's 'Bhooth Bangla' has surpassed the impressive Rs 200 crore milestone globally. Under the direction of Priyadarshan, the film has made waves at the box office, raking in Rs 128.05 crore net from Indian audiences and an additional Rs 52.25 crore from international markets in just two weeks. Read on to know more in detail.

articles.title: Rongorongo Tablets Dated Earlier: Study dates 1 tablet to 1493-1509; independent script claim still unproven
articles.description: A study on Easter Island's Rongorongo script suggests it might be an independent invention, predating European arrival. Researchers' radiocarbon dating of wooden tablets indicates some are centuries older than previously thought. If confirmed, this would place Rongorongo among a rare few writing systems developed without outside influence, potentially rewriting human history and highlighting Rapa Nui intellectual prowess.

articles.title: In 1948, what Walt Disney saw at a Ford Factory changed the way Disneyland was designed
articles.description: Walt Disney's 1948 visit to the Ford River Rouge plant profoundly influenced Disneyland's design. He observed the factory's organized flow of materials and people, a system that inspired his approach to guest experience. This industrial efficiency, with its conveyor belts and logical progression, shaped how Disneyland guides visitors, creating a seamless and immersive theme park journey.

articles.title: In 1948, the McDonald brothers watched a service crisis and reinvented the way we eat
articles.description: McDonald's iconic golden arches weren't a grand design but an everyday observation. Facing chaos and high costs, brothers Richard and Maurice McDonald radically simplified their drive-in menu from 25 items to just nine in 1948. This "reset" created an assembly-line approach, drastically cutting costs and boosting sales, birthing the fast-food model that revolutionized dining globally.

articles.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 arrives in theaters Friday, bringing back Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci to the fashion world that made the 2006 original a pop culture favorite. Early reviews and fan reactions suggest a polished, familiar sequel that leans on its star power, designer aesthetics and a storyline centered on print media struggling to stay relevant in the digital age.

The father-son duo has previously starred in three successful films. Chaitanya also discussed navigating their professional relationship, balancing creative input with Nagarjuna's final decisions as producer. Meanwhile, Chaitanya is also gearing up for his supernatural thriller, 'Vrushakarma'.

articles.title: Akshay Kesari set caught fire due to no safety measures
articles.description: A fire broke out on the set of Akshay Kumar’s 'Kesari' during a 2018 climax shoot, destroying it and causing losses of Rs 8 crore. SFX supervisor Vishal Tyagi said there were no safety measures, and the crew watched helplessly for two hours. No casualties were reported.

articles.title: He became an overnight star after a hit. Suffered brain stroke, Salman Khan paid his medical bills. Now, he is making reels and looking for genuine work
articles.description: Actor Rahul Roy, known for Aashiqui, is back in the spotlight with viral social media reels post-brain stroke. Facing criticism and trolling, he recently defended his work, citing financial needs and pending legal matters. He appealed for genuine work opportunities, emphasising resilience and dignity after his health crisis. Read on to know more about his journey.

articles.title: 'Dhruva Natchathiram' prepares for release; Gautham Menon and Vikram's long-pending film aims for May 27
articles.description: Gautham Menon's long-delayed film 'Dhruva Natchathiram,' starring Vikram, is targeting a May 27 release. Director Menon has submitted details to the court to clear outstanding dues, a crucial step towards resolving financial hurdles. If pending payments are settled promptly, fans can finally anticipate this star-studded project hitting theatres after years of anticipation.
